items.title: Philippines: Rights Advocates in Mining Areas Face Prosecution
items.description: Click to expand Image Climate activists hold up portraits of slain Philippine environmental defenders during the Global Day of Action for Climate Justice protest in Quezon City, Philippines, November 6, 2021. © 2021 Ezra Acayan/Getty Images (Manila) – Prosecutors in the Philippines should dismiss criminal libel complaints, including those filed by a mining company, against four rights advocates in Nueva Vizcaya province, Human Rights Watch said today. In July 2026, the North Luzon Mineral Resources Corporation filed a criminal complaint for cyber-libel against two lawyers representing local communities. The complaint accused the lawyers of defaming the company through their statements on Facebook and on a radio broadcast in which they alleged that the company did not consult residents affected by the company’s operations, including farmers and members of the Indigenous Bugkalot/Ilangot tribe. Two community leaders also face cyber-libel complaints. “Philippine prosecutors should dismiss criminal complaints filed by companies to silence communities raising concerns about their business operations,” said Lian Buan, Southeast Asia researcher at Human Rights Watch. “Philippine legislators should revive bills that would make it easier for the authorities to block such criminal lawsuits from proceeding.” Cyber-libel, defined as libel “committed through a computer system or any other similar means,” is a criminal offense under the Cybercrime Prevention Act of 2012, punishable by 6 to 12 years in prison. Under Philippine law, prosecutors can dismiss criminal complaints, including for libel, for lack of evidence. The lawyers, Fidel Santos and Edgardo Balgos, represent residents of Kasibu, a town in Nueva Vizcaya province. The residents filed a petition on July 14 before the Philippines Department of Environment and Natural Resources to cancel the company’s permit for mining exploration, alleging it had not adequately consulted the communities. The residents said in their petition that they have a Certificate of Land Ownership Award that confirms their land rights as agrarian reform beneficiaries, and that their area is protected watershed under local laws. In the company’s complaint against the lawyers, filed on July 2, the company said that it had consulted with two communities twice in March 2024, before it was granted an exploration permit on June 25, 2025, covering 4,455 hectares encompassing different villages. The company said it has a certification from the Philippine National Commission on Indigenous Peoples that its permit area does not overlap with ancestral domain, a designation under Philippine law for territory used by Indigenous communities since time immemorial. The pattern of criminal libel suits against the community leaders and their legal representatives have the effect of being so-called SLAPP suits, Strategic Lawsuits against Public Participation, Human Rights Watch said. “They are filing cases one after the other,” Santos said. “They targeted the leaders first, and now their lawyers.” The cyber-libel charge against the lawyers has the effect of undermining their right to freedom of expression, and the residents’ right to legal representation during disputes, Human Rights Watch said. “As their lawyer, I have a responsibility to advocate for their cause,” Balgos said. “But this [criminal libel case] creates a chilling effect, because if their defender is being harassed, then the more afraid they are.” A community leader, Florentino Daynos, is also facing a cyber-libel complaint that the company filed on June 19 for his support for a community barricade against mining exploration in Kasibu. On June 3, Daynos and another community leader, Anthony Glenn Guingayan Sr., were notified that other Nueva Vizcaya residents had brought additional cyber-libel complaints against them. The complaints that the company filed directly will undergo preliminary investigation by prosecutors in Isabela province, where the company is based, a four-hour drive from the two lawyers. Filing complaints far from the defendant is a common tactic in libel cases in the Philippines. Companies and Philippine authorities have used cyber-libel charges to intimidate critics. The United Nations special rapporteur on freedom of expression has specifically condemned the use of cybercrime laws to suppress free speech and criticism in the Philippines. The cybercrime law and provisions on libel in the revised penal code seriously undermine the right to freedom of expression under the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights, to which the Philippines is a party. Human Rights Watch opposes criminal defamation laws because they have a serious chilling effect on free speech. The UN special rapporteur on the rights of freedom of peaceful assembly and of association has stated that “SLAPPs aim to shut down critical speech by intimidating critics into silence and draining their resources.” Philippine authorities have not protected activists, lawyers, and others from such lawsuits by companies and state agencies, Human Rights Watch said. The UN special rapporteur on the promotion and protection of human rights in the context of climate change reported in August 2024 that there should be a “major judicial inquiry” in the Philippines “to review all cases in which environmental human rights defenders have been prosecuted on trumped-up charges.” The Philippines has had special rules against SLAPPs since 2010 for environmental cases. However, the rules have not deterred the filing of SLAPPs against Philippine environmental defenders. The Philippine-based Legal Rights and Natural Resources Center has found that “many [environmental defenders] have been detained under false charges.” House Bill No. 5592, filed in October 2022, seeks to expand SLAPP prohibitions to all public interest cases and make it mandatory for prosecutors and courts to determine whether a case is a SLAPP when it is filed and if so dismiss it summarily. The bill has not progressed in Congress. The UN special rapporteur on freedom of expression has also recommended that states enact anti-SLAPP legislation, including early dismissal with awarding costs and other measures to penalize abuse. “Efforts to target Indigenous communities and their lawyers in the courts should prompt firm action by the Philippine government,” Buan said. “If environmental and human rights advocacy is criminalized, affected communities will be unable to sound the alarm when corporations and officials threaten their rights.”

items.title: India: Excessive Force Used Against Student Protesters
items.description: Click to expand Image Police officers use batons to disperse protestors during a march by the Cockroach Janta Party in New Delhi, India, July 20, 2026. © Photo by Kabir Jhangiani/NurPhoto via AP (New York) – Indian security forces used tear gas and batons to suppress largely peaceful student and youth protests in New Delhi on July 20, 2026, Human Rights Watch said today. Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s administration should promptly and impartially investigate unnecessary and excessive use of force, allow peaceful protests, and stop suspending mobile internet services, which put people at added risk. Protests that began in June to demand accountability for mismanagement of medical entrance examinations grew to include grievances against unemployment and government corruption. The “Cockroach Janta Party,” a Gen Z-led movement that began after the Supreme Court chief justice compared unemployed youth to cockroaches, called for a peaceful march to parliament on July 20. Scores of people, including some police officers, were injured, with one young woman protester in critical condition. “India’s youth wanted to march peacefully to parliament to seek accountability from their elected leaders,” said Meenakshi Ganguly, deputy Asia director at Human Rights Watch. “Instead, the police met them with batons, tear gas, and internet shutdowns.” Human Rights Watch interviewed 9 witnesses, including protest organizers, journalists, and student protesters; and analyzed and verified 10 videos posted on social media. On July 21, political opposition leaders criticized the police crackdown, calling the government “insensitive” and “authoritarian.” Delhi police temporarily detained several top opposition leaders after a nearly three-hour protest in front of Modi’s residence. The Supreme Court refused to hear a plea on alleged police abuses against student protesters, saying, “don’t waste our time.” Since June, demonstrators had occupied Jantar Mantar, an 18th century observatory that is a prominent protest site a couple of kilometers from parliament. The protests gained momentum on July 18 after Delhi police forcibly removed the activist Sonam Wangchuk, who had been on a hunger strike since June 28, from Jantar Mantar and admitted him to a public hospital. The Delhi police claimed that protesters displayed “unruly, aggressive and violent behavior,” attacked them with stones and other objects, vandalized their vehicles, and resorted to “large-scale violence,” injuring their personnel. Human Rights Watch verified one video showing protesters beating and piling onto a police officer, who falls to the ground. The Cockroach Janta Party and protesters rejected the police account of large-scale violence, saying the police used force without any provocation. “The protesters were just sitting there, begging the police not to beat them, asking them, ‘Why are you beating us?’” said a 34-year-old Delhi-based lawyer. “This entire crowd was far more engaging with the police than retaliating. No hitting back or stopping the batons, they were just taking the hits.” “I did not expect the police to be so brutal,” said a 24-year-old photojournalist who was covering the protest. “I have photos of protesters just standing there and the police beating them. I saw male police officers bashing up women. The women were entreating with folded hands and still police officers were beating them up.” A number of protesters said that security forces pushed them into very tight spaces, or charged at crowds, risking a stampede. “It was suffocating, there was hardly any space to breathe,” said a 24-year-old law student describing a skirmish that took place when police charged at protesters who had tried to breach police barricades. “Somehow, I managed to keep standing, but two of the girls with me fell and people stepped on them.” Human Rights Watch verified six videos showing security officers using batons or tear gas against protesters, or pelting them with stones. In one video, a possible tear gas canister lands and bursts among a group of protesters sitting on the ground. The force of the blast knocks one protester off his feet as he tries to run away. Human Rights Watch verified a video that shows three men in civilian clothes holding plastic batons like those used by security officers. At least one is seen beating protesters alongside police officers. Several witnesses said police were also present in civilian clothes and many police and Rapid Action Force riot-control police did not wear name tags which thwarts accountability for their actions. According to digital rights groups, protesters were also subjected to surveillance using facial recognition systems. Telecommunication service providers reportedly received orders to shut down mobile internet services in parts of central Delhi on July 20. However, the government did not abide by Supreme Court directions to make suspension orders public. A protester said he lost his friends in the stampede-like situation and then the police hit him with a stick on his elbow. But he could not locate his friends, he said, “because they had shut down mobile internet and jammed mobile services, so no calls were going through.” Indian authorities have a history of shutting down the internet during protests to restrict access to information, conceal abuses by security forces, and prevent independent documentation of these abuses, Human Rights Watch said. Access to the internet is widely recognized as an indispensable enabler of a broad range of human rights guaranteed in the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights and other human rights instruments to which India is a party. The United Nations Human Rights Council passed a resolution in 2016 unequivocally condemning internet shutdowns and called upon all states to “refrain from and cease such measures.” The right to peaceful assembly and protest is a fundamental right protected under international human rights law. The UN Basic Principles on the Use of Force and Firearms by Law Enforcement Officials provide that officers may only use force when strictly necessary. When using force, law enforcement officials should exercise restraint and act in proportion to the seriousness of the offense and to the legitimate objective to be achieved. The 2020 UN guidance on less-lethal weapons in law enforcement provides that tear gas should only be employed when necessary to prevent further physical harm and should not be used to disperse nonviolent demonstrations. “India’s police have a long history of committing serious human rights violations with impunity,” Ganguly said. “The authorities need to promptly and impartially investigate security force abuses against the protesters in Delhi, and appropriately prosecute those responsible, regardless of rank.”

Dado que los cubanos están pasando por enormes dificultades, este impasse no puede prolongarse indefinidamente. La mejor opción para La Habana es llevar a cabo reformas significativas, al tiempo que deja claro a Washington los peligros que entraña una intervención.

items.title: Osun Election: Accord Party, police differ over neutrality ahead governorship poll
items.description: By Shina Abubakar, Osogbo The Accord Party and the Osun State Police Command have disagreed over allegations of bias and neutrality ahead of the August 15, 2026 governorship election in the state. In a statement, the Osun State Chairman of the Accord Party, Pastor Victor Akande, accused the police of failing to maintain neutrality in the discharge of their duties. He alleged that security operatives had been selective in their operations, claiming that some individuals accused of involvement in violent incidents had not been arrested despite allegations against them. Akande also called on the Inspector-General of Police, Kayode Egbetokun, to order an impartial investigation into recent security incidents in the state and ensure that anyone found culpable is prosecuted, irrespective of political affiliation. The Accord chairman further urged the police to uphold professionalism and guarantee a level playing field for all political parties during the election. Responding, the Police Public Relations Officer in the state, DSP Abiodun Ojelabi, dismissed the allegations, describing them as false and misleading. He said the command remained committed to professionalism, impartiality and the rule of law, stressing that it does not provide protection for any individual, political party or criminal group. According to him, police operations are intelligence-driven and based on credible evidence. Ojelabi said arrests were carried out only where there was sufficient evidence to support investigation and possible prosecution, adding that several suspects linked to breaches of public peace had been arrested, while investigations into other reported incidents were ongoing. He also denied allegations of selective arrests, maintaining that all suspects were apprehended based on reasonable suspicion of involvement in criminal activities. The police spokesperson said political affiliation or membership of any group neither confers immunity from investigation nor guarantees arrest. He appealed to political parties, community leaders and residents to refrain from inflammatory statements capable of heightening tension ahead of the election. Ojelabi assured residents that the command would continue to discharge its constitutional responsibility of protecting lives and property and urged members of the public to provide credible information to support ongoing security operations. The governorship election in Osun State is scheduled to hold on August 15, 2026. The post Osun Election: Accord Party, police differ over neutrality ahead governorship poll appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Burkina : un projet alerte sur les dangers de l’hyperconnectivité et appelle à une journée sans écran le 31 juillet
items.description: Burkina : un projet alerte sur les dangers de l'hyperconnectivité et appelle à une journée sans écran le 31 juillet Ouagadougou, 23 juil. 2026 (AIB)- L’Équipe de recherche en éducation aux médias et au numérique (EREMN), promotrice du projet « OFF DAY : 24 h sans écran, 24 h pour moi », a lancé jeudi une campagne de sensibilisation sur les dangers de l'hyperconnectivité, tout en invitant les jeunes à participer à une journée volontaire de déconnexion prévue le 31 juillet 2026 à Durra Institut, à Ouagadougou. Prévue le 31 juillet 2026 à Durra Institut à Ouagadougou, cette journée de déconnexion volontaire comprendra une conférence sur l'hyperconnectivité, des jeux de société, des jeux traditionnels, un karaoké, des quiz et diverses animations destinées à favoriser les échanges en présentiel et le vivre-ensemble. Présentée au cours d'une conférence de presse, l'initiative entend attirer l'attention sur les effets de l'utilisation excessive des écrans, notamment chez les jeunes, et promouvoir un usage plus responsable des outils numériques. La présidente du comité d'organisation, Rayimwendé Rébecca Sankara, a expliqué que le projet est né du constat de la place de plus en plus importante qu'occupent les écrans dans le quotidien des Burkinabè. S'appuyant sur les statistiques de l'Autorité de régulation des communications électroniques et des postes (ARCEP), elle a rappelé qu'au 31 mars 2026, le Burkina Faso comptait 29,79 millions de cartes SIM actives, soit une télédensité de 120,44 %, illustrant la forte progression de l'utilisation des technologies numériques. Selon Mme Sankara, si le numérique constitue un puissant levier de développement, son utilisation excessive peut également entraîner des conséquences sur le bien-être des utilisateurs, notamment la dépendance aux écrans, les troubles du sommeil, la baisse de la concentration, le stress et l'isolement social. Elle a également attiré l'attention sur le Fear Of Missing Out (FOMO), ou la peur de manquer une information ou un événement lorsque l'on n'est pas connecté, un phénomène qui touche particulièrement les jeunes de la génération Z. « Nous voulons opposer au FOMO le JOMO (Joy Of Missing Out), c'est-à-dire la joie d'accepter de manquer certaines choses pour mieux profiter de l'instant présent », a-t-elle déclaré. Le président adjoint du comité d'organisation a précisé que le projet ne vise pas à remettre en cause les technologies numériques, mais plutôt à encourager un usage plus responsable, plus conscient et plus équilibré des outils digitaux. Il a, par ailleurs, annoncé qu'un quiz en ligne est disponible sur la page officielle du projet afin de permettre aux internautes d'évaluer leur rapport au numérique. À l'issue du test, les participants sont classés dans l'une des trois catégories suivantes : « Conscient », « Fragile » ou « Zombie connecté ». Agence d’information du Burkina GIP/yos

Heavy monsoon rains lash Lahore, disrupt routine life
items.description: LAHORE: Heavy monsoon rains caused widespread water accumulation across Lahore on Thursday, disrupting routine life, slowing traffic and creating difficulties for commuters as rainwater remained stagnant on major roads, intersections and underpasses in several parts of the provincial capital. Despite claims by the district administration and the Water and Sanitation Agency (WASA) Lahore that drainage operations were being carried out on an emergency basis, residents faced difficulties due to three to four feet of standing water on streets, roads and submerged underpasses, along with slow traffic movement. The city received 343 millimetres of rain over the past 48 hours, according to the Provincial Disaster Management Authority (PDMA), which warned of possible urban flooding during the second spell of monsoon rains. Rainwater accumulated at more than 130 locations across Lahore, affecting traffic movement in several areas. Low-lying localities, major roads and underpasses remained among the worst-affected points. In DHA Phases 1 to 5, water accumulation disrupted traffic at multiple locations. Around two feet of water accumulated from Defence Mor to A Block Checkpost, slowing the movement of vehicles. The Ghazi Underpass and Masjid Chowk Underpass were closed due to heavy water accumulation, while traffic was diverted to alternate routes. Traffic movement was also affected from Lalak Jan Chowk to LUMS Road, Watain Chowk to LUMS, Main Boulevard DHA and on the route from Masjid Chowk to Ghazi Chowk. Vehicles were diverted through Z Block and other alternate routes due to the closure of underpasses. Other areas reporting water accumulation included Harbanspura, Mall Road, Cooper Road, Lakshmi Chowk and Chouburji Chowk, where WASA teams carried out drainage operations. The rain also exposed recurring urban drainage challenges as water entered houses in low-lying areas of the city, while commuters complained about difficulties travelling through flooded roads and underpasses. Many citizens complained that the authorities had not taken any measures to drain water from their houses, leaving them to remove it from their homes and streets on their own. Citizens also faced delays as vehicles became stranded in rainwater on roads, forcing motorists to move slowly through standing water, while some avoided major roads due to the risk of breakdowns. Several deep sinkholes, measuring around 10 square feet, appeared on three to four roads across the city, including Canal Road, Johar Town Main Boulevard and Bhekay Wala Mor, causing parts of the roads to collapse. Lahore traffic police cordoned off the affected areas and diverted traffic after WASA teams reached the sites. Another sinkhole reappeared on Khayaban-i-Firdousi, where barriers were placed as a precautionary measure and traffic was diverted to alternate routes. The PDMA said that so far 21 people had died and 154 others had been injured in rain-related incidents across Punjab during the ongoing monsoon season. Fifteen deaths occurred due to building collapses, two due to lightning strikes, three due to electrocution and one person drowned. At least 38 houses also sustained partial damage. PDMA Director General Umar Abbas Mela directed district administrations to remain on high alert and warned of urban and flash flooding risks. He advised citizens to avoid weak structures, low-lying areas and contact with electricity poles and wires during rainfall. Meanwhile, WASA Lahore said it had placed its teams on high alert and launched emergency drainage operations. WASA Managing Director Ghufran Ahmed visited several affected areas, including Mall Road, Cooper Road, Lakshmi Chowk and Chouburji Chowk, to monitor rainwater removal operations. He said all disposal stations were operating at full capacity, while additional machinery and staff had been deployed at water accumulation points. More than 21 monsoon emergency relief camps had also been made operational. He said WASA’s 24/7 monsoon control room was monitoring the situation through more than 200 live cameras, including those installed at underpasses across the city. Lahore Deputy Commissioner retired Capt Ali Ijaz also visited Harbanspura and other areas to review drainage operations. He claimed that most major roads and waterlogged points had been cleared in record time, while the remaining low-lying areas would be drained soon. The administration and WASA urged citizens to avoid unnecessary travel during heavy rainfall, stay away from electricity infrastructure, avoid driving through flooded areas and report water accumulation to WASA through its helpline, 1334. However, residents criticised the recurring problem of water accumulation in Lahore, saying that despite repeated monsoon preparations, poor drainage capacity, blocked sewer lines and delays in rainwater removal continued to create difficulties for citizens every year. Meanwhile, Chief Traffic Officer (CTO) Syed Abdul Raheem Sherazi directed officials to cordon off the sinkholes on different roads across the city and take measures to provide alternate routes to citizens for smooth travel. He also deployed traffic officials outside the underpasses to divert traffic. “Rain duty with commitment, professionalism and public safety is their top priority,” he said.

items.title: El petróleo toca los 100 dólares ante la tensión en el mar Rojo
items.description: El ataque a dos petroleros saudíes que transportaban crudo por el mar Rojo añade presión en el mercado y aviva los temores de interrupciones en el suministro energético mundial. El barril de petróleo brent ha llegado a tocar los 100 dólares, su nivel más alto desde finales de mayo después de que los hutíes de Yemen, alineados con Irán, reivindicaran los ataques. Asimismo, el aumento de los costes energéticos eleva las preocupaciones sobre la inflación y penaliza a bonos y Bolsa. En la zona euro, el Banco Central Europeo ha optado por mantener los tipos de interés sin cambios pero ha advertido que “la incertidumbre sigue siendo elevada y el impacto inflacionista de la perturbación energética no se ha materializado aún plenamente”. Seguir leyendo

En su declaración como acusado, Eugenio Pino ya aseguró que Cosidó estaba al tanto de la operación de espionaje al extesorero popular Luis Bárcenas . items.title: Zondoma : La commune de Tougo lance le processus de mise en place du conseil communal consultatif de la jeunesse
items.description: Burkina/Zondoma-Conseil-Consultatif-Jeunesse Zondoma : La commune de Tougo lance le processus de mise en place du conseil communal consultatif de la jeunesse Tougo, 22 juillet 2026 (AIB) – La délégation spéciale de la commune de Tougo a organisé, mercredi 22 juillet 2026, une rencontre d'information et d'échanges avec les jeunes et les responsables des organisations de jeunesse en vue de la mise en place du conseil communal consultatif de la jeunesse. Cette initiative vise à favoriser une représentation inclusive des jeunes dans les instances de concertation de la commune. La rencontre, tenue dans la salle des fêtes de la mairie de Tougo, a été présidée par le président de la délégation spéciale (PDS), Boureima Souley. Elle a connu la présence de la première vice-présidente de la délégation spéciale, Ouedraogo/Belem Relwindé Cécile, du chef du service Référent Opportunité Insertion (ROPI), Karim Ouédraogo, de la secrétaire générale de la mairie, Elisabeth Zinsoni, ainsi que de nombreux responsables d'associations et de structures de jeunesse. Dans son allocution, le PDS a présenté les objectifs du conseil communal consultatif de la jeunesse, ainsi que les conditions de candidature et les critères de sélection des futurs membres du bureau. Il a insisté sur la nécessité pour les organisations de jeunesse de proposer des personnes engagées et crédibles afin de garantir le bon fonctionnement de cette nouvelle instance. « Nous voulons un Conseil communal Consultatif de la jeunesse composée de femmes et d'hommes responsables, dynamiques et engagés, capables de porter les préoccupations des jeunes et d'accompagner le développement de notre commune », a déclaré Boureima Souley. Le premier responsable de la commune a également invité les associations à désigner des candidats jouissant d'une bonne moralité et d'une réputation irréprochable. Selon lui, la qualité des membres déterminera l'efficacité du futur conseil dans le dialogue entre les jeunes et les autorités locales. Pour faciliter la constitution des dossiers de candidature, des fiches individuelles de renseignements ont été remises aux participants au cours de la rencontre. Les autorités communales ont, en outre, lancé un appel à l'ensemble des organisations de jeunesse de Tougo afin qu'elles diffusent largement l'information et encouragent les jeunes à se porter candidats. L'objectif recherché est de constituer un bureau représentatif, inclusif et équilibré, réunissant des femmes et des hommes capables de contribuer à la promotion de la participation citoyenne des jeunes et à la recherche de solutions aux défis auxquels ils sont confrontés. Les candidats ont jusqu'au vendredi 24 juillet 2026 en fin de journée pour déposer ou compléter leur dossier auprès de la mairie de Tougo. Agence d’information du Burkina DNK

items.title: Última hora de los incendios forestales, en directo | Ayuso avisa de que las próximas horas serán “muy complicadas” en el incendio de Villa del Prado
items.description: La presidenta de la Comunidad de Madrid, Isabel Díaz Ayuso, ha advertido este jueves de que las próximas horas del incendio forestal en Villa del Prado, originado en la localidad toledana de Almorox y que ya ha calcinado 890 hectáreas, serán “muy complicadas” debido a las altas temperaturas y el viento. El fuego está ya perimetrado en la zona de Almorox y ha bajado al nivel 1 de gravedad, según ha confirmado la consejera de Desarrollo Sostenible de Castilla-La Mancha, Mercedes Gómez. La vicepresidenta tercera del Gobierno y ministra para la Transición Ecológica, Sara Aagesen, ha puesto cifras a los incendios que asolan España: 108.000 hectáreas quemadas en lo que llevamos de año, cuatro veces más de lo que había ardido el año pasado en estas fechas. Hay activos cinco incendios de “especial relevancia”, según la ministra, en alusión a los de La Mierla y Selas en Guadalajara, Almorox en Toledo y Villa del Prado en Madrid, Castropodame en León y Borgohondo, en Ávila. A estos hay que sumar fuegos en Soria, Segovia y Huelva, como ha recordado la titular de Transición Ecológica. Seguir leyendo

items.title: Palais Mohammed V : un émissaire d’Emmanuel Macron reçu par Mamadi Doumbouya
items.description: Le président guinéen Mamadi Doumbouya, rentré dimanche du 69ᵉ sommet de la Cédéao tenu à Lungi, près de Freetown, a reçu mercredi au Palais Mohammed V l’émissaire du président de la République française Emmanuel Macron. Selon la Direction de la communication et de l’information de la Présidence de la République de Guinée (DCI-PRG), « au cours de cette rencontre, l’émissaire français a salué le retour effectif de la République de Guinée à l’ordre constitutionnel, félicité les autorités pour la bonne tenue des élections législatives et communales, avant de souligner la portée du discours prononcé par le Président Mamadi Doumbouya lors du 69ᵉ Sommet de la CEDEAO à Freetown ». La DCI-PRG indique également que le chef de l’État guinéen a reçu l’ambassadeur du Royaume du Maroc en Guinée, « porteur d’un message de Sa Majesté le Roi Mohammed VI ». « Dans ce message, le souverain marocain adresse ses félicitations au peuple souverain de Guinée et à son Président pour le bon déroulement des élections, tout en réaffirmant sa volonté de consolider davantage le partenariat historique et les liens d’amitié qui unissent Rabat et Conakry », rapporte la Présidence de la République. Ousmane CAMARA L’article Palais Mohammed V : un émissaire d’Emmanuel Macron reçu par Mamadi Doumbouya est apparu en premier sur Mediaguinee.com .

items.title: 3e session ordinaire de la commune Tône 1 : Un budget supplémentaire de 305 540 129 F CFA adopté
items.description: Dapaong, 23 juil. (ATOP) – La 3 è session ordinaire de la commune Tône 1 a pris fin le mercredi 22 juillet à Dapaong. Un budget supplémentaire de 305 540 129 F CFA a été adopté au cours des travaux, ouverts le 13 juillet dernier. Ce budget consacre 57 361 995 CFA au fonctionnement et 248 178 134 F CFA à l’investissement. Il va permettre à la municipalité de réorienter les priorités immédiates et d’exécuter des actions urgentes attendues par les concitoyens en matière d’infrastructures et services sociaux de base, en l’occurrence les travaux d’aménagement du marché local de Dapenkpergou. Outre l’adoption de ce budget, les conseillers municipaux ont planché sur la situation financière du 1 er semestre. Sur les prévisions globales (recettes et dépenses) de 404 426 736 F CFA, les recettes s’élèvent à 202 627 917 F CFA, soit un taux de réalisation de 50,10% et les dépenses à 125 188 686 F CFA (30,85%). Il en ressort un solde positif de 77 439 231 F CFA. Les élus locaux ont également fait une descente sur le terrain pour le suivi de la distribution des avis de taxes et pour sensibiliser les contribuables sur le civisme fiscal. Vue partielle des participants Le maire de la commune Tône 1, Mme Lene Nanwabe, a indiqué que l’analyse de la situation financière du 1 er semestre a permis de voir le chemin parcouru et de mettre en lumière les efforts structurels à fournir pour stabiliser la trajectoire économique de la commune. Mme Nanwabe a déploré le manque de civisme fiscal de la plupart des contribuables. « Malgré la réception de leurs titres d’imposition, beaucoup de contribuables trainent encore à s’acquitter de leurs taxes. Nos administrés réclament chaque jour davantage d’infrastructures, de routes, d’éclairage public et de marchés modernes, mais ils refusent de contribuer à l’effort collectif », a-t-elle fait remarquer. Le maire a rappelé que la mobilisation des recettes locales est la condition indispensable pour réaliser les besoins exprimés par les populations. Elle a insisté sur la nécessité de renforcer la sensibilisation au civisme fiscal. Le secrétaire général de la préfecture de Tône, Dr. Kombaté-Sanwogou Soukipiéba s’est réjoui de la descente des conseillers sur le terrain qui traduit leur volonté d’associer la population à la gestion des affaires communales. Il a invité les conseillers municipaux à toujours œuvrer dans cette dynamique afin de renforcer la cohésion sociale et le développement inclusif pour l’intérêt général et le bien-être des populations. ATOP/TKA/JK/BV

Les autorités administratives et locales, venues constater l'état d'achèvement de l'ouvrage, ont échangé avec les différents acteurs impliqués dans sa réalisation en vue d’une mise en service le plus tôt possible. items.title: 3è session ordinaire de la commune Cinkassé 1 : Les travaux axés l’adoption du budget supplémentaire
items.description: Cinkassé, 23 juil. (ATOP) – Le conseil municipal de Cinkassé 1 a ouvert, le mercredi 22 juillet à Cinkassé, sa 3 è session ordinaire de l’année. Les travaux seront consacrés à la revue à mi-parcours du budget exercice 2026, et à l’analyse et l’adoption du budget supplémentaire de la commune. Cette session de sept jours, permettra de renforcer l’efficacité de l’action municipale, d’améliorer les services rendus aux citoyens, d’assurer une meilleure exécution des investissements en cours et de créer les conditions nécessaires à l’atteinte des objectifs de développement fixés pour l’exercice 2026. Les élus locaux examineront également des stratégies pour améliorer le recouvrement des recettes communales, renforcer la gouvernance financière et consolider la collaboration avec les partenaires techniques et financiers. Les participants Le représentant du préfet de Cinkassé, M. Padanareyou Essodong a exhorté les acteurs municipaux à de bonnes réflexions afin de prendre des décisions dans le but d’améliorer les recettes de la commune. Le maire de Cinkassé 1, Nagnango Koundjo a salué l’engagement des élus locaux, en les invitant à des échanges constructifs afin de répondre efficacement aux attentes des populations. La cérémonie s’est déroulée en présence des autorités politiques, administratives et militaires dont le maire adjoint de la commune Cinkassé 2, Djakalidja Temmé. ATOP/BB/JK/SED

items.title: Ghana Navy, Zambia reaffirm commitment to strengthening defence cooperation
items.description: The Ghana Navy and Zambia have reaffirmed their commitment to deepening bilateral defence cooperation following a courtesy visit by the newly appointed Zambian Defence Attaché to Ghana, Colonel Brian Kandiwa Moyo, to the Naval Headquarters on Thursday, 23 July 2026. The visit provided an opportunity for both sides to reinforce longstanding military ties and explore new avenues of collaboration in support of regional peace, security and development. The Chief Staff Officer at the Naval Headquarters, Commodore Ben Baba Abdul, received Colonel Moyo on behalf of the Chief of the Naval Staff, Rear Admiral Godwin Livinus Bessing. During the meeting, Colonel Moyo formally introduced himself as Zambia’s new Defence Attaché to Ghana and conveyed warm greetings from Zambian President Hakainde Hichilema. He reaffirmed his country’s commitment to strengthening defence collaboration with the Ghana Navy and building on the long-standing relationship between the two nations. Colonel Moyo also commended the Ghana Armed Forces Command and Staff College (GAFCSC) for its significant contribution to the professional development of officers of the Zambian Defence Force. According to him, the institution’s training programmes have enhanced strategic leadership and operational capacity within Zambia’s military. In response, Commodore Abdul praised Colonel Moyo’s predecessor for his role in advancing defence cooperation between Ghana and Zambia, describing the relationship between the two countries as historic and enduring. He expressed confidence that Colonel Moyo would build on the existing partnership and further strengthen military relations between the two countries. Commodore Abdul stressed the importance of sustained engagement to expand cooperation beyond military training, with greater collaboration in areas that promote peace, security and national development. He further assured the Zambian Defence Attaché of the Ghana Navy’s full support in the discharge of his diplomatic and military responsibilities and wished him a successful tenure in Ghana. The engagement reflects the continued commitment of both Ghana and Zambia to strengthening defence diplomacy and fostering closer military cooperation as part of broader efforts to enhance regional stability and security.

items.title: PFIPC: Daddy Freeze, journalist urge evidence-based scrutiny
items.description: Media personality Ifedayo Olarinde, popularly known as Daddy Freeze, and journalist Fegiro Johnson have called for evidence-based public discourse regarding allegations involving the Chief of Staff to the President, Femi Gbajabiamila, urging Nigerians to allow due process and avoid drawing premature conclusions. Their comments come amid an ongoing investigation by the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) into allegations made by Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi concerning the purported Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC). The Presidency has maintained that the PFIPC is not a recognised government agency and has no legal backing. In a video shared on social media, Daddy Freeze questioned the basis of the allegations, citing reports that Adeyemi said he never met Gbajabiamila in person and dealt only through an alleged intermediary. According to him, allegations of financial misconduct should ordinarily be supported by verifiable evidence, such as communication records, bank transaction details or other documentary proof. He said public opinion should be guided by facts established through investigation rather than speculation, adding that photographs with public officials do not, on their own, constitute evidence of wrongdoing. Daddy Freeze stressed that his comments should not be interpreted as a defence of any individual but as a call for adherence to due process and evidence-based discussions. Similarly, journalist Fegiro Johnson urged Nigerians to distinguish between allegations and proven facts, warning against allowing political sentiments to influence public judgment before investigations are concluded. In a post on X, Johnson argued that claims of financial transactions should be supported by credible documentary evidence and maintained that the matter should be assessed on the basis of verifiable facts. The comments have generated debate on social media, with differing opinions expressed over the allegations and the ongoing investigation. Gbajabiamila has denied any wrongdoing, stating that he has no involvement in the allegations. The ICPC has not concluded its investigation, and no findings have been announced. The controversy arose following allegations made by Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi relating to the purported Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC). The Presidency has stated that the PFIPC is not a government agency established under Nigerian law. The ICPC subsequently commenced an investigation into the allegations. The investigation remains ongoing, and no court or law enforcement agency has found Gbajabiamila or any other person liable for any offence in connection with the matter. The post PFIPC: Daddy Freeze, journalist urge evidence-based scrutiny appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Mahama’s future plans remain an NDC matter—Gbande
items.description: The Deputy General Secretary of the National Democratic Congress (NDC), Mustapha Gbande, says any decision regarding President John Dramani Mahama’s future will be determined through the party’s internal structures and not public discussions. Speaking on JoyPrime on Wednesday, 22 July, Mr Gbande questioned the certainty of claims that President Mahama would not contest again, stressing that such matters are not discussed outside the party’s established channels. Responding to the claim that President Mahama would not contest again, Mr Gbande asked, “How did you know that he’s not contesting anymore?” He said President Mahama does not communicate important party matters outside the party’s internal meetings and that NDC executives would be informed if the president intended to make any announcement regarding his plans. “The president doesn’t speak to the party outside the party boardroom. We are his party executives,” Mr Gbande said. He explained that President Mahama’s position as leader of the NDC means that discussions about his plans involve the party leadership. “The president didn’t make himself; the party made him. He’s a leader of the party. That conversation I’ve indicated many times—that is not a conversation for anybody. It’s a conversation for a certain class of individuals,” he stated. Mr Gbande added that the NDC remains central to decisions concerning its leader, noting that President Mahama’s views are important in determining the party’s direction. “We control him. He’s our leader. He’s the leader of the party. What the leader of the party says, plus what he thinks, is what takes place,” he said. He, however, suggested that discussions about future electoral decisions are premature, pointing out that President Mahama is still early in his current term. “He’s tired. But we are not there yet. The man is just two years in power,” Mr Gbande added. Mr Gbande’s comments come amid discussions about whether President Mahama will seek another term, but he maintained that such matters will be addressed through the appropriate structures of the NDC.

items.title: Gnagna : le secrétaire général de la province lance l’épreuve théorique d’admissibilité du CSAPé à Bogandé
items.description: BURKINA–GNAGNA–ÉDUCATION–EXAMEN Gnagna : le secrétaire général de la province lance l'épreuve théorique d'admissibilité du CSAPé à Bogandé Bogandé, 21 juillet 2026 (AIB)-Le Secrétaire général de la province de la Gnagna, Mendien dit Moussa Soma, a procédé, mardi à Bogandé, à l'ouverture officielle des plis contenant les sujets de l'épreuve théorique d'admissibilité du Certificat supérieur d'aptitude pédagogique (CSAPé), donnant ainsi le coup d'envoi des examens professionnels des professeurs d'école dans la province. Le cérémonial s'est déroulé au centre unique de Bogandé, aménagé à l'École primaire publique de Tambousgou, en présence des autorités administratives, des membres du jury et des surveillants, dans le strict respect des procédures garantissant la confidentialité, la transparence et la crédibilité de l'examen. À cette occasion, le Secrétaire général de la province, Mendien dit Moussa Soma, a, au nom du Haut-Commissaire de la Gnagna, invité les candidats à faire preuve de sérénité, de concentration et d'intégrité tout au long des épreuves. « La Sirba est en tête des résultats aux examens scolaires au Burkina Faso. Nous comptons également sur vous pour maintenir cette dynamique en réalisant d'excellentes performances », a déclaré M. Soma. Selon les statistiques officielles, 151 candidats, dont 47 femmes et 104 hommes, étaient inscrits à cette session. Au total, 146 candidats, composés de 46 femmes et 100 hommes, ont effectivement pris part à l'épreuve, tandis que cinq candidats, dont une femme et quatre hommes, étaient absents, soit un taux d'absence de 3,31 %. Avant le début des compositions, l'appel des candidats a été effectué à partir de 6 h 30. L'accès aux salles d'examen était subordonné à la présentation de la Carte nationale d'identité burkinabè (CNIB), afin de permettre la vérification de l'identité de chaque candidat. Les surveillants ont ensuite rappelé les principales consignes relatives à la discipline, à l'interdiction de toute forme de fraude et au respect scrupuleux des règles régissant les examens professionnels, dans le souci de garantir des compositions équitables et apaisées. Réservé aux professeurs d'école remplissant les conditions requises, le CSAPé constitue une étape majeure dans l'évolution de leur carrière. Cet examen permet d'évaluer leurs compétences pédagogiques et professionnelles en vue de l'obtention du certificat, ouvrant ainsi des perspectives d'avancement dans la fonction enseignante. À travers l'organisation de cette session, le ministère en charge de l'Éducation réaffirme son engagement à promouvoir le mérite, à valoriser les compétences des enseignants et à contribuer au renforcement de la qualité du système éducatif burkinabè. Agence d'information du Burkina

items.title: Media, INEC, voters hold key to credible 2027 elections — Prof. Akhaine
items.description: …Stakeholders advocate renaming LASU after Lateef Jakande By Olasunkanmi Akoni Media practitioners, the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) and Nigerian voters have a shared responsibility to ensure credible elections in 2027, a professor of Political Science at Lagos State University (LASU), Sylvester Akhaine, has said. Akhaine made the assertion while delivering the keynote address at the 4th Annual Lateef Jakande Memorial Lecture organised by the Nigerian Guild of Editors (NGE) on Wednesday in Ikeja, Lagos. The lecture, themed “Media, INEC, Voters and the Path to Credible Elections,” is held annually in honour of the late Alhaji Lateef Jakande, former Governor of Lagos State and veteran journalist. Drawing lessons from the Jakande era during the Second Republic, Akhaine described the lecture theme as timely in view of preparations for the 2027 general elections. “For credible elections to take place in our country, the media, INEC and voters all have important roles to play,” he said. He described Nigeria’s democratic landscape as one characterised by “state capture” and “negative sovereignty,” where the executive dominates other arms of government while non-state actors exercise control over parts of the country, worsening insecurity and increasing dependence on local vigilante groups alongside conventional security agencies. Akhaine also criticised the country’s political culture, citing monetised politics, mediocrity in leadership recruitment and a widening trust deficit that has left many citizens disengaged from the democratic process. According to him, the media remain vital to democracy through information dissemination, public education and opinion shaping, noting that Nigeria’s press has historically maintained a strong libertarian tradition dating back to the colonial era. On electoral administration, he traced the evolution of Nigeria’s election management bodies from the Electoral Commission of Nigeria (ECN) to the present-day INEC, established in 1998. He observed that although election management bodies expanded globally following the third wave of democratisation, many across Africa, including Nigeria’s, still lack full institutional independence. Describing voters as the “transmission belt for consent,” Akhaine said they remain central to any democratic process. He noted that while credible elections are defined in different ways, they are most easily recognised by the absence of electoral malpractice such as ballot stuffing, vote-buying, gerrymandering and voter intimidation. Akhaine cited international instruments, including the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights (ICCPR), the African Charter on Human and Peoples’ Rights, the ECOWAS Protocol and the SADC Principles and Guidelines Governing Democratic Elections, all of which promote free, fair and transparent elections. Reviewing Nigeria’s electoral history, he argued that none of the seven general elections conducted since 1999 could be regarded as completely free and fair. He referenced the disputed 1964/1965 elections, the “two-thirds” constitutional controversy of 1979, the annulled June 12, 1993 presidential election and the 2007 general election, which the Commonwealth Observer Group said fell below acceptable standards. He also warned that the media now faces fresh challenges from digital technology, misinformation and post-truth politics, where bots and coordinated online campaigns increasingly undermine factual reporting. Calls for collaboration Akhaine urged greater collaboration among the media, INEC and the electorate to strengthen Nigeria’s democracy. He said the media must remain committed to truth and continue to uphold Sections 22 and 39 of the 1999 Constitution, which empower the press to hold government accountable and guarantee freedom of expression. He urged INEC to maximise its technological reforms and financial autonomy to conduct credible elections, while also supporting the recommendations of the Justice Mohammed Uwais Electoral Reform Committee, particularly the proposal to remove the President’s power to appoint INEC leadership and transfer it to the legislature. He also called for sustained voter education to help citizens resist financial inducements and make informed electoral choices. “If these critical stakeholders play their respective roles, it is my conviction that Nigeria’s democratic journey will be steered in the right direction,” Akhaine said. At the event, stakeholders also advocated renaming Lagos State University after the late Lateef Jakande in recognition of his contributions to education and the establishment of the institution. The post Media, INEC, voters hold key to credible 2027 elections — Prof. Akhaine appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: I was almost killed for speaking to South African woman — Returnee
items.description: By Enitan Abdultawab A Nigerian returnee from South Africa, Jamyou Sanni, has recounted how he narrowly escaped death after he was allegedly ambushed by a group of South African men outside a bar during his stay in the country. Sanni shared the experience while speaking on Vanguard’s The Nigerian Metre Podcast, where he reflected on the xenophobic attacks and misconceptions Nigerians face in South Africa. According to him, the attack happened after a woman approached him while he was relaxing at a bar, an encounter that appeared to provoke some South African men. “I have been attacked in every situation possible. I was in a bar one day when a lady came close to me. You know, just a situation where a lady would come close to you because she sees that you are chilling,” he said. “Unknown to me, the South African guys waited for me outside. When I stepped out, they approached me and smashed me to the floor.” Sanni said he might not have survived the attack if not for the intervention of a white South African police officer. ‘South Africans almost killed me because they saw me chilling with a lady in a bar — Returnee’ Nigerian shares his near-death experience in a xenophobic attack. Watch the full episode on Thursday on our YouTube channel. #xenophobia #southafrican pic.twitter.com/nXQWaV8KBV — Vanguard Newspapers (@vanguardngrnews) July 22, 2026 “It was another South African, a white man who is a police officer, that shot from his apartment upstairs, and that made them disperse. They could have killed me that day,” he added. Despite his experience, Sanni stressed that not every South African is hostile towards Nigerians, saying many residents treated him well during his years in the country. “Not all South Africans are aggressive or hostile,” he said. He also dismissed the common claims that Nigerians take jobs or women from South Africans. “It’s not true that we take their jobs or women. Throughout my stay, I was never employed. I rather employed people,” Sanni said. Speaking on relationships, he argued that many South African women are attracted to Nigerian men because of how they are treated. “As for the women part, more South African women want to get Nigerian men because Nigerian men take care of them more than South African men,” he said. On allegations that foreigners deprive locals of employment opportunities, Sanni maintained that jobs are available for those willing to work. “As for the job, they just do not want to work. If they want to work, they can get a job. It is sad that what they do now is what they think is a job — chasing foreigners away,” he added. He further claimed that women generally occupy a stronger social position than men in South Africa, describing them as “superior than men” within the society. The post I was almost killed for speaking to South African woman — Returnee appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Bobo-Dioulasso : le PDS Laurent Kontogon obtient les bénédictions du chef de canton pour deux projets communaux
items.description: Bobo-Dioulasso : le PDS Laurent Kontogon obtient les bénédictions du chef de canton pour deux projets communaux Bobo-Dioulasso, 23 juil. 2026 (AIB) – Le président de la Délégation spéciale (PDS) de la commune de Bobo-Dioulasso, Laurent Kontogon, a été reçu le mercredi 22 juillet 2026, par le chef de canton de Bobo-Dioulasso qui lui a donné ses bénédictions pour deux initiatives majeures de la commune : la deuxième édition de la Coupe communale de la relève sportive et le lancement d’une web télévision communale, dénommée Bobo-Dioulasso TV. Selon le PDS, cette démarche s'inscrit dans le respect des valeurs coutumières et de la tradition qui consiste à requérir les bénédictions de la chefferie avant toute activité d'envergure. Il a indiqué que cette pratique a toujours porté ses fruits lors des précédentes initiatives de la commune. Évoquant la deuxième édition de la Coupe communale de la relève sportive, Laurent Kontogon a rappelé que la première édition avait connu un franc succès grâce, selon lui, aux bénédictions du chef de canton. C'est pourquoi il a jugé nécessaire de renouveler cette démarche avant le lancement de la nouvelle édition. Le PDS a également présenté le projet de Bobo-Dioulasso TV, une web télévision destinée à renforcer la communication institutionnelle de la commune et à mieux informer les populations sur les actions menées par l'administration communale. À l'issue de l'audience, le chef de canton de Bobo-Dioulasso a accordé ses bénédictions aux deux projets et formulé des vzœux de succès pour leur réalisation. Il a par ailleurs salué les initiatives entreprises par la commune, affirmant suivre avec intérêt les actions engagées en faveur du développement de la ville. Agence d’Information du Burkina OSAR/wis

items.title: Livestream: Finance Minister presents 2026 Mid-Year Budget Review
items.description: Finance Minister Dr Cassiel Ato Forson is in Parliament to present the 2026 Mid-Year Budget Review to Parliament today, Thursday, July 23, outlining the government’s fiscal performance during the first half of the year and its economic policy direction for the remaining months of 2026. The review is expected to assess the economy’s performance against the targets set in the 2026 Budget, which sought to transition Ghana from macroeconomic stabilisation to sustained economic growth while maintaining fiscal discipline. Since the budget was presented in November 2025, key economic indicators have shown stronger-than-expected performance, with inflation declining to 5.3 per cent, alongside improvements in fiscal consolidation, the external sector and debt sustainability. The Finance Minister is also expected to provide updates on revenue mobilisation, public expenditure, debt management and financing plans for the second half of the year. The government is widely expected to maintain its current tax policy, with indications suggesting the review will not introduce new taxes but will instead focus on implementing existing fiscal measures to preserve macroeconomic stability. The review is also anticipated to update Parliament on Ghana’s transition from the International Monetary Fund’s Extended Credit Facility programme to the Policy Coordination Instrument (PCI), which is expected to guide economic reforms after the current IMF programme ends. The Mid-Year Budget Review is regarded as a key fiscal policy statement because it provides insight into government spending priorities, borrowing plans and the broader economic outlook. Businesses, investors and development partners are expected to monitor the presentation closely for signals on inflation, interest rates, exchange rate stability and any adjustments to expenditure allocations or financing strategies for the rest of the year. Watch the event below:

items.title: Déclaration des devises en Algérie : ce qui change pour les voyageurs
items.description: L’Algérie a fixé, par arrêté ministériel publié au Journal officiel ce jeudi 23 juillet, de nouvelles modalités de déclaration des devises par les voyageurs résidents ou non-résidents. Le texte, qui a également défini un modèle de déclaration, adapte les règles aux nouvelles exigences en matière de blanchiment d’argent et de lutte contre le terrorisme. L'obligation de déclaration concerne les « billets de banque, les pièces de monnaie et tous les moyens de paiement au porteur, les effets de commerce, les autres valeurs et titres de créance négociables, au porteur ou endossables, ainsi que les métaux précieux et les pierres précieuses, détenus par les voyageurs », précise l'arrêté signé par le ministre des Finances Abdelkrim Bouzred. La déclaration de la monnaie doit être effectuée, « par écrit », par les voyageurs à l'occasion de leur entrée ou de leur sortie du territoire national, auprès du bureau des douanes d'entrée ou de sortie. Les voyageurs souscrivent la déclaration de la monnaie, « essentiellement, par voie électronique avant l'arrivée au bureau des douanes. ». L’article 7 de l'arrêté oblige les voyageurs non-résidents exportant des montants importés et non utilisés en Algérie, à présenter, à la sortie du territoire national, auprès du bureau des douanes la déclaration de la monnaie souscrite à l'entrée, visée par un guichet de la Banque d'Algérie, un guichet d'une banque intermédiaire agréée et/ou un bureau de change « constatant les opérations de change effectuées durant leur séjour en Algérie. ». Autre nouveauté introduite par cet arrêté : les agents des douanes peuvent demander aux voyageurs « toutes informations ou tous documents jugés utiles relatifs, notamment à l'origine et à la destination des montants et des valeurs transportés. ». Déclaration des devises par les voyageurs : l’Algérie adopte sa législation Selon le même arrêté, les Douanes algériennes « constituent des bases de données » des déclarations de monnaies, nationale et étrangères, reprenant toutes les informations figurant sur les déclarations de monnaie souscrites par les voyageurs. Ces données comprennent l’identité et l'adresse du voyageur en Algérie ou à l'étranger, les documents de voyage en possession du voyageur, les montants des monnaies et des valeurs déclarés, le lieu de souscription de la déclaration, la destination et la provenance du voyageur, l'origine et la destination des montants et des valeurs déclarés et le bénéficiaire et le destinataire effectifs des montants et des valeurs déclarés, selon la même source. Les bases de données ainsi constituées font « l'objet d'échanges et d'exploitation dans le cadre de la coopération nationale et internationale. » « Toutes les données relatives aux déclarations de la monnaie souscrites et celles relatives aux fausses déclarations et aux cas de non-respect des obligations déclaratives commises par les voyageurs, sont échangées avec l'organe spécialisé dans la lutte contre le blanchiment d'argent et le financement du terrorisme », précise encore le texte. L’Algérie adapte ainsi sa réglementation sur l’exportation des devises par les particuliers à la nouvelle loi sur la lutte contre le blanchiment d’argent et le financement du terrorisme qu’elle a adoptée en 2025. L’article Déclaration des devises en Algérie : ce qui change pour les voyageurs est apparu en premier sur TSA .

items.title: Here’s the latest.
Détournement présumé de 511 milliards GNF : la défense de l’ex-ministre de la Défense Mohamed Diané plaide la relaxe
items.description: Comme nous l’annoncions dans un précédent article, le procureur spécial près la Cour de répression des infractions économiques et financières (CRIEF) a requis ce jeudi 23 juillet 2026 une peine de 10 ans d’emprisonnement, une amende de 50 milliards de francs guinéens contre Dr Mohamed Diané, ainsi que la saisie de ses biens, de ceux de son épouse et de ses enfants. Il a également demandé de donner mandat à l’AGRASC pour gérer les biens saisis et d’ordonner la publication de l’arrêt à intervenir dans un journal. Ces réquisitions ont été prises contre l’ancien ministre de la Défense, poursuivi pour « détournement de deniers publics, enrichissement illicite et blanchiment de capitaux » portant sur un montant de 511 milliards de francs guinéens. Sur le chef de « détournement de deniers publics », le ministère public, représenté par Biwon Millimouno, a évoqué les 500 milliards de francs guinéens de crédits alloués au ministère de la Défense, département que dirigeait Dr Mohamed Diané, également ordonnateur principal du budget. Selon le magistrat, le prévenu n’a pas été en mesure de justifier l’utilisation de ce montant. Le procureur spécial a également évoqué les 11 milliards de francs guinéens représentant le contrat d’acquisition de véhicules (pick-up) conclu entre Djoma Groupe SA et le ministère de la Défense. Avec un ton ferme, le magistrat a estimé que la demande de comparution de l’intendant général formulée par le prévenu au cours des débats constituait « une fuite en avant », rappelant que celui-ci avait pour mission d’assurer la logistique. Abordant les infractions liées à « l’enrichissement illicite et au blanchiment de capitaux », le procureur spécial a attiré l’attention de la Chambre des appels, présidée par Daye Mara, sur l’inadéquation entre les ressources du prévenu et son salaire. Il a notamment fait référence au patrimoine immobilier de Dr Mohamed Diané, de son épouse Aminata Touré ainsi que de ses enfants M’Mawa Diané (étudiante) et Ahmed Sékou Diané (âgé de 14 ans), qui auraient acquis des terrains au quartier Nongo. Les 900 millions de francs guinéens de salaires décaissés au nom et sur le compte d’un certain Mohamed Diané, magistrat à la Cour suprême, n’ont pas été occultés par le procureur spécial dans ses réquisitions. Pour toutes ces raisons, il a réitéré sa demande de condamnation de l’ancien ministre. Des arguments balayés par la défense, qui estime que les faits de « détournement de deniers publics, enrichissement illicite et blanchiment de capitaux » reprochés à son client ne sont pas établis. Dans leurs plaidoiries, Me Sidiki Bérété et Me Almamy Samory Traoré ont tenté de démontrer que Dr Mohamed Diané n’a jamais géré 11 années d’exercices budgétaires pour utiliser un fonds évalué à 500 milliards de francs guinéens. Concernant les 11 milliards évoqués par le parquet, les avocats ont expliqué que le contrat conclu entre le ministère de la Défense et Djoma Groupe SA avait fait l’objet d’un appel d’offres restreint, en raison de l’urgence liée à la livraison des pick-up destinés à l’armée. Par ailleurs, Me Sidiki Bérété a fustigé l’insertion des montants de 511 milliards de francs guinéens dans le dossier de la procédure ainsi que la demande de justification, par son client, du budget de souveraineté d’Alpha Condé, alors président de la République. Avant de solliciter la relaxe de son client, l’avocat s’est interrogé : Qui détenait le chéquier du ministère de la Défense ? Qui établissait les engagements ? Qui procédait au décaissement des salaires ? Quel est le ministre qui a comparu ici sans son comptable ? Des interrogations faisant allusion à l’intendant général du ministère de la Défense nationale, dont la comparution a été sollicitée tout au long des débats par le prévenu. Pour conclure sa plaidoirie, Me Bérété a affirmé qu’aucun document comptable n’avait été présenté à la Cour pour prouver le détournement de deniers publics attribué à Dr Mohamed Diané. Dans le même élan, il a demandé à la Cour de constater l’absence de rapport d’inspection et de reconnaître que le contrat conclu entre le ministère de la Défense et Djoma Groupe SA avait été passé dans les règles de l’art. En plus de plaider la non-culpabilité de son client, la défense a sollicité que la Cour statue dans un délai raisonnable. Lébéré Baldé L’article Détournement présumé de 511 milliards GNF : la défense de l’ex-ministre de la Défense Mohamed Diané plaide la relaxe est apparu en premier sur Mediaguinee.com .

items.title: Burkina: 520 nouveaux sergents prêts à servir la Nation
items.description: Sous la présidence du Ministre d'État, Ministre de la Guerre et de la Défense patriotique, le Général de Division Célestin SIMPORÉ, s’est tenue la cérémonie officielle de fin de formation de la 14 ᵉ promotion des El è ves sous-officiers sp é cialistes ce mercredi 23 juillet 2026 à l'École Nationale des Sous-Officiers d'Active (ENSOA), au camp Bila ZAGRÉ de Kamboinsin. En présence des autorités militaires, paramilitaires, administratives, coutumières, religieuses et diplomatiques, ainsi que des parents et amis des stagiaires, ces derniers fort de 520 nouveaux sergents dont 125 femmes ont été baptisée « Promotion Sergent-Chef Kaboré Tilado » et placée sous le parrainage du Lieutenant à la retraite Zouré Albert. Le ministre d'Etat, ministre de la Guerre et de la Défense patriotique, Général Célestin Simporé Regroupant 43 spécialités, la promotion a suivi une formation de sept mois articulés autour d'une phase d'adaptation, de la formation militaire initiale et de la certification interarmées, sanctionnée par l'obtention du Certificat Toutes Armes n°2, attestant de l'aptitude des stagiaires à conduire un groupe de combat. Au nom de ses camarades, le Sergent DIBLONI Sansan Augustin, délégué de promotion, a remercié les autorités militaires et l'encadrement pour la qualité de la formation reçue. Il a affirmé la disponibilité des nouveaux sous-officiers à servir partout où le devoir les appellera et s'est engagé, avec ses camarades, à faire vivre la devise de l'ENSOA : « s’élever par le savoir et l'excellence pour servir. » Dans son allocution, le Chef d'État-Major de l'Armée de Terre, le Colonel ROUAMBA Hamed Hermann, a rappelé que le galon de sergent : « consacre l'entrée dans le corps des chefs de proximité, responsables de la formation, de l'encadrement et du maintien du moral des hommes ». Il a exhorté les nouveaux Sous-Officiers à faire preuve d'un patriotisme exemplaire, d'une loyauté sans faille, d'intégrité, de discipline et de résilience, en plaçant toujours l'intérêt supérieur de la Nation au-dessus de toute autre considération. Pour sa part, le Commandant de l'ENSOA, le Chef de bataillon BIKIENGA Ali, a exprimé sa reconnaissance aux autorités militaires, au personnel d'encadrement ainsi qu'à l'ensemble des acteurs ayant contribué au succès de cette formation. Il a également salué la bonne collaboration des populations riveraines du camp et le soutien de la République sœur du Niger à travers la mise à disposition de personnel d'encadrement. Le parrain de la promotion, le Lieutenant à la retraite Zouré Albert, a félicité ses filleuls et les a exhortés à mériter la confiance de leurs chefs. Il leur a rappelé que, « les techniciens que vous êtes sont avant tout des soldats », avant d'exprimer sa gratitude au commandement pour l'honneur qui lui a été fait de parrainer cette 14ᵉ promotion. Clôturant la cérémonie, le Ministre d'État, Ministre de la Guerre et de la Défense patriotique, a invité les nouveaux sous-officiers à demeurer professionnels, loyaux, fidèles à leur serment et dignes du nom de leur promotion. Il a rappelé que le Sergent-Chef Kaboré Tilado fut un combattant discipliné, déterminé et engagé jusqu'au sacrifice suprême pour un Burkina Faso libre, indépendant et souverain. Il a également rendu un vibrant hommage à l'ensemble des Forces combattantes ainsi qu'au peuple burkinabè pour leur engagement constant en faveur de la souveraineté nationale. Le Sergent-Chef Kaboré Tilado, valeureux combattant tombé sur le champ d'honneur lors d'une mission au détachement de Bahn a fait preuve d'un courage exceptionnel en protégeant ses frères d'armes au péril de sa vie. Son engagement, son esprit de sacrifice, sa discipline et son ardeur au travail constituent désormais les valeurs de référence de cette jeune promotion. Les meilleurs stagiaires distingués au cours de la cérémonie sont entre autres, le major de promotion, le Sergent NITIEMA Franck Wendpouiré Rodrigue, qui a obtenu la moyenne de 16,97/20, et le major féminin, le Sergent NITIEMA Wendkouni Fidèle Josiane, avec une moyenne de 16,82/20. DCRP/MGDP L’article Burkina: 520 nouveaux sergents prêts à servir la Nation est apparu en premier sur Wakat Séra .

items.title: UK releases list of items travellers cannot bring into the country in 2026
items.description: The United Kingdom, UK has published an updated list of prohibited and restricted items that travellers are not allowed to bring into the country in 2026, warning that banned goods will be seized by customs officials at the border. The updated guidance, issued as part of the UK’s border and customs regulations, outlines items that are either completely prohibited or require special licences before they can be imported. According to the guidance, several goods are banned from entering the UK because they pose risks to public safety, public health, the environment and wildlife conservation. Among the prohibited items are controlled drugs, offensive weapons such as flick knives, self-defence sprays including pepper spray and CS gas, endangered animal and plant species, rough diamonds, and indecent or obscene materials, including certain books, magazines, films and DVDs. The UK government also bans personal imports of meat and dairy products from most non-European Union countries in a bid to prevent the spread of animal diseases. In addition to banned goods, authorities said certain items can only be brought into the country if travellers obtain the necessary permits or licences beforehand. These include firearms, explosives and ammunition, all of which are subject to strict import controls. Food and plant products are also subject to regulations. Authorities said such items may be refused entry if they are not free from pests and diseases, are intended for commercial use rather than personal consumption, or were not grown within the European Union. The guidance also warns against bringing counterfeit or pirated goods into the UK. Customs officials may seize items suspected of infringing intellectual property rights, including unauthorised copies of films, music and other copyrighted materials, while offenders could face prosecution. UK authorities advised travellers to familiarise themselves with the country’s customs regulations before travelling to avoid delays, confiscation of goods or possible legal consequences upon arrival. List of items travellers cannot bring into the UK in 2026 Prohibited items Controlled drugs Offensive weapons, including flick knives Self-defence sprays, including pepper spray and CS gas Endangered animal and plant species Rough diamonds Indecent or obscene materials, including certain books, magazines, films and DVDs Personal imports of meat and dairy products from most non-EU countries Restricted items (special licence required) Firearms Explosives Ammunition Food and plant products subject to restrictions Food and plant products may be refused entry if they: Are not free from pests and diseases Are intended for commercial rather than personal use Were not grown in the European Union Counterfeit and pirated goods Travellers are also prohibited from bringing goods that infringe intellectual property rights, including: Pirated copies of films Illegal copies of music Counterfeit branded products Other unauthorised copyrighted materials UK customs authorities warned that prohibited items will be seized, while travellers found importing restricted goods without the required documentation may face prosecution. Vanguard News The post UK releases list of items travellers cannot bring into the country in 2026 appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: N319m Fraud: Appeal Court rejects jailed ex-FCTA Director’s plea for bail.
items.description: By Ikechukwu Nnochiri, Abuja The Abuja Division of the Court of Appeal has dismissed an application filed by a convicted former Director of Finance and Administration in the Federal Capital Territory Administration (FCTA), Mr Garba Mohammed Dukku, seeking his release from prison custody. Dukku, who worked under the Abuja Metropolitan Management Council (AMMC) of the FCTA, is serving a 24-year prison term for diverting public funds to the tune of N319 million. He was found guilty by the Federal High Court in Abuja on charges bordering on corruption and money laundering. Trial Justice James Omotosho found him guilty on a six-count charge marked FHC/ABJ/CR/608/2022, filed against him by the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC). The ICPC had established that the convict, between 2012 and 2013, fraudulently diverted a total sum of N318,250,000 belonging to the AMMC into his personal Fidelity Bank account in various tranches, including N56.25 million, N71 million, N53 million, N54 million, N46 million, and N36.3 million. The Commission further proved that the diverted funds were subsequently transferred to bureau de change operators for unauthorised purposes. Although Mr Dukku had, during the trial, claimed that the funds were handed over to his superiors, his defence was dismissed by the court, which pointed out the lack of credible evidence to support it. As an alternative to the prison term, the trial court gave the convict the option of paying a fine equivalent to five times the amount involved in each count of the charge, which would amount to about N1.6 billion. Meanwhile, the convict, who had since commenced his prison term, approached the Court of Appeal to be released on bail pending the determination of an appeal he lodged against his conviction and sentence. In dismissing the bail application, the appellate court held that nothing was placed before it to sway its discretion in favour of the convict. In a unanimous decision by a three-member panel of justices, the appellate court held that the applicant had failed to show any exceptional circumstance to warrant his release from prison custody. It therefore dismissed the bail request for want of merit. However, the appellate court granted an accelerated hearing of the substantive appeal filed by the convict. The post N319m Fraud: Appeal Court rejects jailed ex-FCTA Director’s plea for bail. appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Killer whales: Orcas blow fish to bits 'for fun', researchers say
items.description: A group of killer whales have been documented blowing a sunfish to bits in an unusual underwater escapade offering never-before-seen insights into orca behaviour, according to research released on Thursday. Video released by the US-based NGO Beneath The Waves shows one orca holding the tail of a sunfish, with its fellow whale then charging with full force into the rotund mola, which explodes into pieces. The sunfish is already dead — this isn’t an elaborate murder ritual. Instead, it might be an attempt to turn the fish into smaller bite-sized chunks, researcher Kathryn Ayres said. “Or it could also just be for fun. Orcas are known for playing with their food,” she explained. “Orcas often tear apart prey and share it with other members of the group, including calves and juveniles.” The antics were documented on two occasions in 2024 and 2025 off the Gulf of California, according to the research published in the peer-reviewed Frontiers in Ethology . Mark Peter Simmonds, director of science at OceanCare, likened it to “human formal meals where socialising as well as nutrition are in play”. “Perhaps it is similar to the time when the lead human at a formal meal would carve up the roast,” he told AFP . Killer whales are highly intelligent apex predators and have been known to play with their prey and deploy sophisticated tactics to ensnare them. They have been documented engaging in “wave washing” — cooperating to create choppy waters that knock seals off sheets of ice so they can be more easily captured and eaten. They have also been seen wearing dead salmon as hats for reasons that remain a mystery. And they use tools — research last year showed a critically endangered population of orcas in the Salish Sea breaking off pieces of seaweed to rub and groom each other. “It just really shows us how intelligent orca are,” Rebecca Wellard, a scientist at Western Australia’s Curtin University who was not involved in the research, told AFP . “I think I hope that gives people more respect for complex creatures that are great predators in their environment.” The hapless sunfish can weigh up to 2,000 kilogrammes and is distinct for both its size and peculiar shape featuring a flattened body and fins. They don’t commonly explode, the research points out. “They’re a heavy, bony fish, so it must be some severe ramming,” Wellard said.
